tool.name: record_coverage
tool.description: Record or update an attack-surface coverage item in the shared assessment ledger. Use this to track what has been tested, what remains open, and what is blocked with evidence.
tool.details: The root agent should initialize coverage early for high-value targets/components/surfaces. Repeated calls with the same target/component/surface update the existing record instead of creating duplicates.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The specific asset, service, or repository area being assessed.

parameter.name: component
parameter.description: The feature, subsystem, route group, or logical component.

parameter.name: surface
parameter.description: The concrete attack surface to cover, e.g. "admin order endpoints", "JWT verification path", "file upload parser".

parameter.name: status
parameter.description: One of: uncovered, in_progress, covered, not_applicable, blocked.

parameter.name: rationale
parameter.description: Why this status is correct right now, including evidence or blocker details.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Priority of this surface: low, normal, high, critical.

parameter.name: next_step
parameter.description: The next concrete action required to move this surface forward.

tool.name: bulk_record_coverage
tool.description: Create or update multiple coverage items in one call. Prefer this at the start of a scan to seed the ledger with the main attack surfaces.

parameter.name: items
parameter.description: A JSON array of objects. Each object should contain target, component, surface, status, rationale, and optionally priority and next_step.

parameter.name: preserve_existing_status
parameter.description: When true, existing coverage items keep their current status, rationale, priority, and next_step instead of being reset by the new seed input. Default: false.

tool.name: record_hypothesis
tool.description: Track an active security hypothesis so the team can reason systematically instead of relying on transient chat history.

parameter.name: hypothesis
parameter.description: The suspected issue or attack path to validate.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The asset or target area this hypothesis applies to.

parameter.name: component
parameter.description: The feature or subsystem relevant to the hypothesis.

parameter.name: vulnerability_type
parameter.description: The vulnerability class, e.g. idor, sqli, ssrf, business_logic.

parameter.name: status
parameter.description: One of: open, in_progress, validated, rejected, blocked.

parameter.name: priority
parameter.description: Priority of the hypothesis: low, normal, high, critical.

parameter.name: rationale
parameter.description: Supporting reasoning, signals, or blocker details.

tool.name: record_evidence
tool.description: Store durable evidence tied to the assessment ledger. Use this for strong signals, confirmed observations, failed but informative attempts, or payload outcomes worth preserving.

parameter.name: title
parameter.description: A short evidence title.

parameter.name: details
parameter.description: The evidence details, observation, or result.

parameter.name: source
parameter.description: One of: code, runtime, traffic, tool, research, user, other.

parameter.name: target
parameter.description: The asset or target area this evidence belongs to.

parameter.name: component
parameter.description: The feature or subsystem this evidence belongs to.

parameter.name: related_coverage_id
parameter.description: Optional coverage item ID linked to this evidence.

parameter.name: related_hypothesis_id
parameter.description: Optional hypothesis ID linked to this evidence.

tool.name: list_assessment_state
tool.description: List the current shared assessment ledger, including coverage, hypotheses, evidence, and finish readiness.

parameter.name: include_resolved_coverage
parameter.description: When false, only unresolved coverage items are returned.

parameter.name: include_evidence
parameter.description: When true, include recent evidence records.

parameter.name: max_items
parameter.description: Maximum number of coverage, hypothesis, and evidence items to return.
